ucs.title: [LLM] Cleaver (TG-2889) ARP cache poisoning AiTM on internal segments
ucs.description: Hunts for execution of ARP-poisoning toolchains (Cain & Abel, ettercap, arpspoof) used by Cleaver/Operation Cleaver operators to perform Adversary-in-the-Middle attacks against internal infrastructure (energy, oil & gas, airlines) — a defining TTP of this Iranian cluster per the Cylance Operation Cleaver report.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("cain.exe","abel.exe","ettercap.exe","arpspoof.exe","bettercap.exe","netcommander.exe") OR Processes.process="*arpspoof -i*" OR Processes.process="*ettercap*-M arp*" OR Processes.process="*bettercap*arp.spoof*" OR Processes.process="*Cain.exe*") by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval risk_score=case(match(process_name,"(?i)cain|abel"),90,match(process_name,"(?i)ettercap|bettercap|arpspoof"),85,1=1,60) | where risk_score>=60 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - risk_score
ucs.kql:
let arpTools = dynamic(["cain.exe","abel.exe","ettercap.exe","arpspoof.exe","bettercap.exe","netcommander.exe","intercepter-ng.exe"]);
let cmdMarkers = dynamic(["arpspoof -i","ettercap -T -M arp","bettercap","arp.spoof on","-M arp:remote"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName in~ (arpTools)
    or ProcessCommandLine has_any (cmdMarkers)
    or (FileName =~ "powershell.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Invoke-ARPSpoof","Get-NetNeighbor","-NeighborState Reachable"))
| join kind=leftouter (DeviceNetworkEvents | where Timestamp > ago(30d) | summarize internalDsts=dcount(RemoteIP), internalPorts=make_set(RemotePort,50) by DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId=ReportId) on DeviceId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FolderPath, SHA256
| extend ActorContext = "G0003 Cleaver / TG-2889 — Iranian, Operation Cleaver Cylance 2014"
ucs.rationale: ARP cache poisoning was a hallmark of Operation Cleaver — the Cylance report documented Cleaver operators using Cain & Abel and similar AiTM tools to harvest credentials laterally inside ICS-adjacent corporate networks. Most APTs prefer LSASS/Kerberoast over Layer-2 attacks, so a hit on these toolnames or arp-spoof command lines on a corporate or OT-edge endpoint is a strong actor-specific signal

ucs.title: [LLM] Cleaver credential harvesting: Cachedump / PWDump / LSASS dump on critical-infrastructure hosts
ucs.description: Detects the post-exploitation credential-dump pattern Cleaver/TG-2889 ran after gaining a foothold — custom-built and off-the-shelf dumpers (Cachedump, PWDumpVariant, gsecdump, Mimikatz) executed against LSASS / SAM / cached domain creds, often staged to %TEMP% or %ProgramData% prior to PsExec lateral movement.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("cachedump.exe","gsecdump.exe","pwdump.exe","pwdump7.exe","wce.exe","mimikatz.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") OR Processes.process="*sekurlsa::logonpasswords*" OR Processes.process="*lsadump::cache*" OR Processes.process="*lsadump::sam*" OR Processes.process="*reg save*hklm\\sam*" OR Processes.process="*reg save*hklm\\security*" OR Processes.process="*procdump*-ma*lsass*" OR Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*MiniDump*") by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as src_host | join type=left src_host [| tstats summariesonly=t count as psexec_count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("psexec.exe","paexec.exe","psexesvc.exe") OR Processes.process="*\\admin$\\*" by Processes.dest | rename dest as src_host | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | eval cleaver_chain=if(psexec_count>0,"YES","NO") | where cleaver_chain="YES" OR count>=2 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let dumpTools = dynamic(["cachedump.exe","gsecdump.exe","pwdump.exe","pwdump7.exe","wce.exe","mimikatz.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe"]);
let cmdMarkers = dynamic(["sekurlsa::logonpasswords","sekurlsa::tickets","lsadump::cache","lsadump::sam","reg save hklm\\sam","reg save hklm\\security","reg save hklm\\system","procdump -ma lsass","comsvcs.dll, MiniDump","-accepteula -ma lsass"]);
let dumps = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where FileName in~ (dumpTools)
        or ProcessCommandLine has_any (cmdMarkers)
        or (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump")
    | project DumpTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, DumpProcess=FileName, DumpCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ParentProc=InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FolderPath, SHA256;
let lateral = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where FileName in~ ("psexec.exe","paexec.exe","psexesvc.exe")
        or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("\\admin$\\","net use","-accepteula -s -d");
dumps
| join kind=leftouter (lateral | summarize psexecHits=count(), psexecCmd=any(ProcessCommandLine) by DeviceId) on DeviceId
| extend ActorContext = "G0003 Cleaver / TG-2889 — Iran-nexus credential harvesting chain (Cylance Operation Cleaver, Dell SecureWorks TG-2889)"
| project DumpTime, DeviceName, AccountName, DumpProcess, DumpCmd, ParentProc, FolderPath, SHA256, psexecHits, psexecCmd, ActorContext
| order by DumpTime desc
ucs.rationale: Cylance and Dell SecureWorks documented that Cleaver/TG-2889 operators dropped a recurring kit — Cachedump, PWDumpVariant, gsecdump, and Mimikatz — and chained the dump with PsExec for lateral movement on energy, aerospace and airline networks. The chain (dump tool + PsExec on the same host within a tight window) is far more specific to this cluster's tradecraft than firing on Mimikatz alone, whic
